summary: Tested subscription/trial/pricing system and auth upsert fix. All 14 backend tests passed. Frontend Pricing page works correctly. Fixed a critical AdminPage runtime crash due to wrong field name (stats.subscriptions vs stats.subscription_breakdown).

minor.issue: Duplicate return statement in /api/subscription/status (dead code after first return). Lines 53-59 are unreachable but harmless.

action_items: Remove dead duplicate return block in /app/backend/routers/subscription.py lines 53-59 (cosmetic fix)
Run supabase_migration_v3.sql in Supabase Dashboard to enable trial columns and pricing_plans table
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: AdminPage.js crashed with 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' due to referencing stats.subscriptions instead of stats.subscription_breakdown (the API field name). Fixed by changing Object.entries(stats.subscriptions) to Object.entries(stats.subscription_breakdown) with null guard.
subscription.py has duplicate return statement (lines 53-59 are dead code after the first return on line 45)

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Pricing page renders correctly, Coming Soon modal works, Admin Pricing tab works after fix

context_for_next_testing_agent: All new features working. Auth upsert fix confirmed (no .select() chain issue). Pricing page at /pricing has 3-column layout with £0/£9.99/£19.99, Coming Soon modal on Get Basic/Get Premium. Admin Pricing tab shows migration message. Admin tier dropdown includes free/trial/basic/premium. TrialBanner component exists and loads from /subscription/status. Migration v3 SQL still not run - trial columns and pricing_plans table absent, backend handles gracefully with fallbacks.
rca of the issue: AdminPage was calling Object.entries(stats.subscriptions) but /api/admin/stats returns subscription_breakdown not subscriptions. This caused a TypeError crash on admin load. Fixed by updating the key reference and adding a null guard.
